commands.review_summary:
The branch adds Telegram topic-name cache writes for forum-topic create/edit actions and reads cached topic names into native slash-command agent context.

PR surface: Source +34. Total +34 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main has topic-name cache reads/writes for normal inbound messages but not native commands, and the PR's new action/native paths can be shown from source to use mismatched cache scope/key choices; no live runtime repro was run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs a contributor or maintainer patch update plus real Telegram proof; ClawSweeper should not queue repair while the external real-behavior proof gate is still missing.

target.title: fix(telegram): propagate forum topic names into agent context
target.body:
## Summary

Forum topic names are tracked in the topic-name-cache via `forum_topic_created`/`edited`/`closed` events in `bot-message-context`, but this metadata was not surfaced in two key code paths.

**Native command handler** (`bot-native-commands.ts`): Builds the agent context payload with `IsForum: true` but never looked up the cached topic name. Agents had no way to know *which* forum topic they were responding in.

**Action runtime** (`action-runtime.ts`): Executes `createForumTopic` and `editForumTopic` actions but never persisted the resulting topic metadata back to the cache. Subsequent messages in newly created or renamed topics could not resolve the name until the next `forum_topic_created`/`edited` service message arrived.

## Changes

1. `bot-native-commands.ts`: Look up the cached topic name via `getTopicName` and include `TopicName` in the agent context payload.
2. `action-runtime.ts`: After `createForumTopic` success, persist the new topic name (and icon metadata) to the cache. After `editForumTopic` success, persist the updated fields.

## Verification

- `pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/topic-name-cache.test.ts`: 13/13 pass
- `pnpm check:changed`: all lanes green (typecheck, lint, import cycles)
- Pre-existing failures in `bot-message-context.topic-agentid.test.ts` confirmed on clean `origin/main` (unrelated to this change)

Closes #86024
